title: Anthropic buys Stainless, the toolmaker behind every major AI company
desc: Daily AI news satire. Anthropic acquires Stainless, the small dev-tools vendor that quietly generated the SDKs and MCP connectors used by OpenAI, Google, Cloudflare, Replicate, Runway and Anthropic itself.

beats.name: two-rivals-two-blades
beats.narration: For years, the major AI companies disagreed about almost everything, except one quiet vendor.

beats.name: the-neutral-anvil
beats.narration: The vendor was Stainless, a small developer tools company founded in 2022 that generated the libraries those companies use to ship their AI to the world.

beats.name: pichai-with-his-scroll
beats.narration: OpenAI used it. Google used it. Cloudflare, Replicate, Runway. And Anthropic.

beats.name: the-hooded-buyer-arrives
beats.narration: The work was the kind of thing almost no one notices, the connectors, command-line tools, and plumbing that let an app actually call an API.

beats.name: the-banner-changes
beats.narration: The deal was not officially priced. Reports cite a figure of more than three hundred million dollars.

beats.name: the-gates-close
beats.narration: The hosted product that rivals had been using to generate new libraries is being wound down.

beats.name: rivals-return-find-it-closed
beats.narration: The companies racing the hardest against Anthropic now share, briefly, a small administrative problem.

beats.name: the-blades-they-have
beats.narration: The libraries already generated for those customers will keep working.

beats.name: the-smith-works-on-inside
beats.narration: The founding team, and the engine that made those libraries, are joining Anthropic.

beats.name: rivals-walk-down-the-mountain
beats.narration: Their plumbing was being built by a neutral shop on the side of the road. Anthropic just bought the shop.

beats.name: the-amber-banner-empty-courtyard
beats.narration: It is a small bill, for a quiet company. It is also a reminder that in the AI race, the most valuable real estate may not be the model. It may be the gate it ships through.

sources.attribution: Anthropic has acquired the dev tools startup used by OpenAI, Google, and Cloudflare (TechCrunch)
Anthropic acquires Stainless to strengthen Claude's developer tooling (InfoWorld)
Anthropic Acquires Stainless, Shuts Hosted SDK Tools (Winbuzzer)
Anthropic buys Stainless, forcing OpenAI and Google to rebuild or migrate SDK tooling (DigiTimes)

transcript: For years, the major AI companies disagreed about almost everything, except one quiet vendor. The vendor was Stainless, a small developer tools company founded in 2022 that generated the libraries those companies use to ship their AI to the world. OpenAI used it. Google used it. Cloudflare, Replicate, Runway. And Anthropic. The work was the kind of thing almost no one notices, the connectors, command-line tools, and plumbing that let an app actually call an API. This week, Anthropic acquired Stainless. The deal was not officially priced. Reports cite a figure of more than three hundred million dollars. The hosted product that rivals had been using to generate new libraries is being wound down. The companies racing the hardest against Anthropic now share, briefly, a small administrative problem. The libraries already generated for those customers will keep working. The founding team, and the engine that made those libraries, are joining Anthropic. Their plumbing was being built by a neutral shop on the side of the road. Anthropic just bought the shop. It is a small bill, for a quiet company. It is also a reminder that in the AI race, the most valuable real estate may not be the model. It may be the gate it ships through.

writing.author: Claude
